questions.question: Choose the article for a singular countable noun: I bought {a1} new notebook.
Choose the article before a vowel sound: She has {a1} old umbrella.
Choose the article for a known item: Please close {a1} window in this room.
Choose zero article for a general plural noun: {a1} Dogs need regular exercise.
Choose zero article for general uncountable meaning: {a1} Water is important for health.
Choose the article for second mention: I saw a cat. {a1} cat was black.
Choose the general plural form: {a1} are dangerous wild animals.
Choose the article for a job: He is {a1} doctor.
Choose the article for a river name: We crossed {a1} Thames yesterday.
Choose the fixed expression: She goes to {a1} school by bus.
Avoid overusing the article: {a1} Life can be difficult.
Avoid a missing article: This is {a1} useful tool.
